proof_limits: checker output and local smokes are support evidence only
regression_risks: The offline checker decodes JWT header and claims and pins the openssl subprocess argv, but it does not cryptographically verify the generated signature against the throwaway public key; a non-empty invalid signature would not be rejected by the decoded-shape assertions alone.

If BRICK_DASHBOARD_SA_KEY_PATH is present but unreadable or malformed, header construction raises before a ReportSinkObservation is returned; this is fail-closed, but status-class observation for that failure path is not proven.

The runtime signer writes private key bytes to a temporary PEM file and unlinks it after subprocess completion; no repo/log/return leak was observed, but crash-time temp-file residue behavior was not proven.
The actual RSA/temp checker and full profile are temp-directory dependent, so this read-only sandbox did not execute the highest-risk runtime signing proof.
remaining_delta: Run the focused dashboard_productization_projection/read_side_projection_boundary checker in a writable temp-capable environment and record the green result plus Authorization-removal RED observation.
Run the required full gate in a temp source copy after bake_dashboard_data_json in that copy and state the copy path.
Consider adding offline RSA signature verification with the throwaway public key if the checker is intended to prove signed-JWT validity, not only header/claim shape and subprocess argv.
No Link Movement is chosen by this QA return.
